title: board-superpowers stage registry
description: JSON Schema validating scripts/stages-registry.yml. Per ADR-0014 (3-artifact pattern: YAML metadata + Python helpers + JSON Schema validation). Catches enum typos, missing required fields, malformed `applicable_when` predicates, malformed `target_state_schema` shapes, and dangling `depends_on` references (cross-stage validation lives in scripts/stages_lib/_lifecycle.py — JSON Schema cannot easily express). Vocabulary anchors: ADR-0027 § 2 (`kanban_projection_capability` predicate; supersedes the pre-#70 dispatch model retired by ADR-0027); ADR-0024 § Part A (settings.yml family + four localities); ADR-0023 (5-element config item protocol — `interactive_prompt` + `validation_kind`); ADR-0020 (3 `applicable_when` forms).

meta.description: Optional registry-level metadata. Per ADR-0021 — schema_version is per-file (registry as a whole) AND per-stage-target (declared inside each stage's `target_state_schema_version`).

schema_version.description: Registry-file schema version. Bumped when the *registry's own shape* changes (a new top-level field, a new required column, etc.) — independent of any individual stage's `target_state_schema_version`.

stages.description: The stage entries. Order is informational — runtime ordering is computed from `depends_on` (topological sort) by scripts/stages_lib/_lifecycle.py.

interactivePromptOption.description: A single literal option for single-choice or multi-choice prompts when `options_source: literal`. The `value` must correspond to a value accepted by the parent stage's `target_state_schema`.

value.description: Machine-readable value; MUST match a value in the parent stage's `target_state_schema` enum (for single-choice) or be a list element (for multi-choice). This is the value persisted to settings.yml.

label.description: Human-readable display label shown to the architect in the prompt.

description.description: Optional one-sentence explanation of what choosing this option means.

stage_id.description: Globally unique identifier. Per design doc § Stages: `<module>.<scope>.<verb-noun>`. `<scope>` is `host` or `repo` (matching `locality`). `<verb-noun>` may carry sub-segments (e.g., `m7.repo.inject-block.routing-rule`). Module number is 1..10. The per-stage Python helper lives at `scripts/stages_lib/<stage_id_slug>.py` where slug = stage_id with `.` and `-` replaced by `_` (e.g., `m4.repo.apply-audit-ddl` → `m4_repo_apply_audit_ddl.py`). Per ADR-0014 § Decision, the runtime path is derivable from `stage_id` via this slug rule — no separate path field is required.

stage_name.description: Human-readable short name for the architect-facing Stages table (design doc § Stages column 2).

description.description: One-sentence prose description (design doc § Stages column 3).

module.description: Functional module identifier (per ADR-0014 + design doc § Column / field semantics + phase-2-foundation.md T2.1). Values `M1`..`M10`. The settings.yml family persists each stage's `target_state` projection at `modules.<module_section_path>` (overridable via `module_section_path` per ADR-0021).

character.description: Axis A — execution character (per ADR-0014 + design doc § Column / field semantics). `automated` = SKILL computes target_state without architect input. `agentic` = stage requires architect input via 5-element config item protocol (ADR-0023).

locality.description: Axis B — locality (per ADR-0014 + ADR-0024 § Part A). Where target_state is persisted. `host-shared` = `~/.board-superpowers/settings.yml`. `repo-shared` = `~/.board-superpowers/repos/<repo-identity>/settings.yml` (HOST-side, gitignored at host level — per ADR-0024 § Path mappings). `repo-git` = `<repo>/.board-superpowers/settings.yml` (committed). `repo-clone` = `<repo>/.board-superpowers/settings.local.yml` (gitignored). `external` = observed via API (RDBMS / GitHub Project / etc.) — NOT persisted; observed via target_state_predicate live IO subject to `external_ttl_seconds` cache.

platforms.description: Per ADR-0016 cross-platform parity contract. `both` is the default. `cc` is an alias for `cc-only`; `codex` is an alias for `codex-only`. The hook applies `platforms` BEFORE `applicable_when` (ADR-0016 § Composition with `applicable_when`); a stage filtered out by `platforms` does not appear in any lifecycle state on the wrong platform.

flags.description: Free-form flag set documenting non-functional traits the lifecycle / hook may treat specially (e.g., `heavy` = take longer than a typical stage; `network-required` = needs internet; `confirm-only` = agentic stage that frequently confirms a sensible default).

introduced_in_version.description: Plugin version that first shipped this stage's *semantic work* (not when the registry-modeled stage_id concept was introduced). Used by the lifecycle model to detect cohort introduction.

deprecated_in_version.description: Plugin version that retires this stage. Recorded entries flip to `deprecated` lifecycle state — preserved as history but no longer drive execution. Optional.

executor.description: Architect-facing executor description (design doc § Stages column 11; per ADR-0014 SoT field). For automated stages: `scripts/<script>.sh` or `scripts/stages_lib/<stage_id_slug>.py`. For agentic SKILL-routed stages: `SKILL: bootstrapping-repo` or compound form. Note: the runtime Python callable path is derivable from `stage_id` via the slug rule (per ADR-0014 § Decision) — this field carries the architect-facing display string, not a separate runtime path.

generation.description: K8s-style monotonic integer (Layer 1 of three-layer fingerprint per ADR-0013). Bumped when *any* aspect of the expected target changes (schema, executor, content template). Layer-1 fast-path for the lifecycle hook.

depends_on.description: Stage_ids that must be `completed` before this stage runs (per ADR-0014 SoT field name). Forms an explicit DAG; topological sort by scripts/stages_lib/_lifecycle.py. Cross-stage existence + acyclicity validation happens at lifecycle load — JSON Schema only checks pattern conformance here.

target_state_schema.description: JSON-Schema-style declaration of the shape `compute_target_state()` returns. Free-form object; the lifecycle validates each entry's recorded `target_state` against this schema at load time. Authoring guide: design doc § 'Per-stage entry shape' (lines 607-660 of redesign doc) — DO NOT invent shapes; mirror what the executor actually computes.

target_state_schema_version.description: Schema version of the `target_state` field's *own shape* (per ADR-0013). Bumped when the stage author changes the structure of `target_state` itself (additive only, lazy-on-read). Independent of registry-file `meta.schema_version` and per-stage `generation`.

default_value.description: For `character: agentic` stages: the default value the prompt-renderer pre-fills (ADR-0023 element 1 + element 3). Architect may accept the default to advance. Type matches `target_state_schema`'s declared shape (e.g., int for `numeric-range`, string for `single-choice`). Optional for automated stages.

validation_kind.description: Per ADR-0023 element 3 (Interaction). One of the five supported `interactive_prompt` kinds. REQUIRED when `character: agentic`; SHOULD be omitted when `character: automated` (no architect input).

interactive_prompt.description: Per ADR-0023 element 3. Generic prompt-renderer reads this object and produces the architect-facing question. SHOULD be present whenever `character: agentic`. When `options_source: literal`, the `options` array MUST be populated with structured option objects whose `value` fields map to values in the parent stage's `target_state_schema`.

prompt.description: User-facing prompt template. May reference enum option list / numeric range from `target_state_schema`.

options_source.description: Where the option list comes from when `kind` is `single-choice` or `multi-choice`. `literal` = structured `options` array in this object. `computed-from-schema-enum` = derived from parent stage's `target_state_schema` enum at render time. `runtime-derived` = computed by Python callable at runtime.

options.description: Structured literal option list. REQUIRED when `options_source: literal`. Each option's `value` MUST correspond to a value in the parent stage's `target_state_schema` enum (for single-choice) or be a permitted list element (for multi-choice). The generic prompt-renderer uses these to produce a machine-parseable choice list.

re_prompt_trigger.description: Per ADR-0023 element 5. One-sentence description of when this stage re-prompts. The mechanism is always lifecycle-derived (generation bump → stale → re-prompt; manual settings-file edit fails schema → stale → re-prompt; `scripts/bsp-stage-rerun.sh <stage_id>` forces never-run). The text here documents the *trigger condition* the architect should expect.

hash_excluded_fields.description: Dot-paths within `target_state` that are stripped before `target_state_hash` computation (per design doc § 'Hash-allowlist (fields excluded from hash)'). Default per-stage exclusions (`last_validated_at`, `last_run_id`, `external_validated_at`, `external_ttl_seconds`) are auto-applied — list only stage-specific additions.

external_ttl_seconds.description: Cache TTL for `locality: external` stages. The lifecycle's Layer-3 live IO (target_state_predicate) re-runs only when wall-clock time exceeds `external_validated_at + external_ttl_seconds`. Default convention: 86400 (24h). REQUIRED when `locality: external`. MUST NOT appear on non-external stages.

applicable_when.description: Per ADR-0020 conditional applicability predicate. Three legal forms: (1) setting-path declarative; (2) kanban-projection-capability declarative (per ADR-0027 § 2 — supersedes the pre-#70 dispatch model); (3) Python escape hatch. Predicate evaluates false → stage returns `not-applicable`. Cheap to evaluate (hook-side); never blocks for IO.

setting_path.description: Dotted path into the merged settings (e.g., `m10.repo.choose-kanban-projection.target_state.kanban_projection`).

equals.description: Value the setting_path must equal for the predicate to evaluate true.

kanban_projection_capability.description: Per ADR-0027 § 2. Capability name as declared in the active projection's reference file under `skills/operating-kanban/references/<projection-id>.md § 'Setup capabilities'`. Lowercase-kebab-case. Resolved at evaluation time by reading `<repo>/.board-superpowers/settings.yml § modules.m10_kanban.<kanban-id>.projection`, loading the matching reference file, and checking whether the named capability is declared. Predicate true → stage runs; false → `not-applicable`.

kanban_id.description: Per ADR-0026 multi-kanban schema. Kanban id whose projection is consulted. Defaults to `primary` when omitted.

python.description: Per ADR-0020 escape hatch. Dotted import path to a callable returning bool. LAST RESORT — every Form-3 use accumulates registry-side complexity. Document the rationale in the per-stage Python module's docstring.

module_section_path.description: Per ADR-0021. Override for the `target_state` projection target inside the settings.yml file. Default is `modules.<lower(module)>` (e.g., `modules.m4_audit` for an M4 stage). Override when the architect-facing concept name differs from the internal module name.

kind.description: M7-only field per ADR-0018 multi-stage routing-block protocol. `required` blocks must be present in the routing target file; `optional` blocks are opt-in.

block_max_bytes.description: M7-only field per ADR-0018. Per-block byte budget. The 4 KiB single-block cap and 32 KiB total Codex AGENTS.md budget are the canonical caps.

then.description: external-locality stages MUST declare external_ttl_seconds (live IO cache TTL).

else.description: non-external-locality stages MUST NOT declare external_ttl_seconds.

then.description: agentic stages MUST declare validation_kind + interactive_prompt + default_value per ADR-0023 5-element protocol.
M7 inject-block stages MUST declare kind + block_max_bytes per ADR-0018.
